target_audience.details: Professional truck drivers on Facebook. Primary goal: awareness and tune-in for the weekly Facebook Live event. Secondary goal: conversion to Outriders Drivers Club free membership. Drivers who are opinionated about the industry, skeptical of marketing, and respond to authenticity and insider access. The positioning deliberately challenges drivers who think they already understand trucking — the hook works on anyone with an opinion about the industry who has not yet heard what Jeff is building. New audience growth beyond existing followers. Broad Facebook reach, US national.

campaign_goal.primary_goal: Build a recurring weekly audience for Jeff Swenson's Facebook Live show and convert viewers into Outriders Drivers Club free members. Position Jeff as a founder-level authority on the future of trucking infrastructure — not a trucker content creator.

key_messages: You don't know 'what the truck's going on' until you've heard the bold new plan from Jeff Swenson, Founder & CEO of LineHaul Station.
Tune in for Trucker Tuesdays and take your seat at the founder's table as Jeff shares bold new ideas and real infrastructure that will change how you think about the future of trucking.
This isn't another trucker show — it's a founder building new infrastructure live, and drivers get a real voice in what comes next through the Outriders Drivers Club.

cta.text: Tune in live — Trucker Tuesdays with Jeff Swenson. Every Tuesday at 6PM.
cta.lead_capture_point: Facebook Live — LineHaul Station page. Secondary CTA: Outriders Drivers Club free membership link (TBD — not yet confirmed).

budget.notes: Organic weekly promo posts at no cost. Optional paid Facebook boost on high-performing episode clips — test $50–100 per boost before committing to recurring spend. Creative production: near-zero (static graphics only, produced by Anj).

creative_assets.title: Trucker Tuesdays — Main Weekly Promo Graphic (Square)
creative_assets.notes: APPROVED. Square format. Features Jeff with ghosted truckers-watching-the-show background. Trucker Tuesdays with Jeff Swenson logo lockup. Chrome and carbon fiber palette. Fuel orange accents. Bottom bar: Facebook Live icon + blank date/time field (Anj swaps weekly) + Tune In Live indicator. This is the weekly announcement template — only the date/time field changes each week. Do not redesign week to week.

creative_assets.title: Trucker Tuesdays — Pull Quote Graphic (Square, Evergreen)
creative_assets.notes: IN PRODUCTION. News/editorial style. Layout: Trucker Tuesdays logo across top full width. Jeff image left 40% (chest-up, no laptop, natural expression). Pull-quote block right 60% with ghost LHS network map at very low opacity. Pull quote text: 'You don't know what the truck's going on until you've heard the bold new plan.' Attribution below: Jeff Swenson, Founder & CEO, LineHaul Station. Supporting line: 'On Trucker Tuesdays, Jeff invites drivers to the founder's table to share bold new ideas and real infrastructure that will change how you think about the future of trucking.' Bottom: Facebook Live | Tuesdays. NO date/time slot — this is an evergreen authority piece, not an episode promo. Once complete: use as introduction post, pin to top of Facebook page, and repurpose on LinkedIn.

creative_assets.title: Trucker Tuesdays — Clean Chrome Alternate (Square)
creative_assets.notes: DONE. Clean chrome and carbon fiber only, no truckers-watching background. Jeff image centered and crisp. Same logo lockup. Use for variety rotation or when episode tone calls for more formal/premium treatment.

show_identity.host: Jeff Swenson, Founder & CEO, LineHaul Station
show_identity.positioning_statement: Trucker Tuesdays is not a trucking content show. It is a founder-level briefing for drivers — Jeff Swenson opening the playbook on how trucking infrastructure is being rebuilt, in real time, and talking directly to the people it is being built for.
show_identity.approved_hook: You don't know 'what the truck's going on' until you've heard the bold new plan from Jeff Swenson, Founder & CEO of LineHaul Station.
show_identity.approved_full_description: You don't know 'what the truck's going on' until you've heard the bold new plan from Jeff Swenson, Founder & CEO of LineHaul Station. Tune in for Trucker Tuesdays and take your seat at the founder's table as Jeff shares bold new ideas and real infrastructure that will change how you think about the future of trucking.
show_identity.approved_formal_description: Trucker Tuesdays with Jeff Swenson is not industry commentary — it's a founder opening the playbook on how trucking is being rebuilt in real time. Jeff Swenson, Founder & CEO of LineHaul Station, shares bold new ideas, flex-based terminal infrastructure, and a fundamentally different vision for the future of trucking — explained directly to drivers while it's being built. This is your chance to sit at the founder's table, understand the ideas behind the decisions, and hear what's coming before forming another opinion about the industry.
show_identity.approved_pull_quote: You don't know what the truck's going on until you've heard the bold new plan.
show_identity.pull_quote_attribution: Jeff Swenson, Founder & CEO, LineHaul Station
show_identity.pull_quote_supporting_line: On Trucker Tuesdays, Jeff invites drivers to the founder's table to share bold new ideas and real infrastructure that will change how you think about the future of trucking.
show_identity.approved_linkedin_variant: You don't understand where trucking is headed until you've heard what's being built. — Jeff Swenson, Founder & CEO, LineHaul Station
what_this_show_is: A founder-level briefing for drivers — not a talk show, not entertainment
Jeff Swenson building real infrastructure live and explaining it directly to drivers
New information — not recycled industry opinions or hot takes
Access to decisions before they are final
A direct bridge to the Outriders Drivers Club and driver participation in what is being built
what_this_show_is_not: A sales pitch dressed as a show
The same opinions every other trucking voice is sharing

core_themes.description: How LineHaul Station infrastructure is actually planned, financed, and rolled out — explained to drivers before anyone else hears it.
Why the old model does not scale and what replaces it: flex-based terminals, hub-and-spoke relay, driver-first infrastructure.
Decisions explained directly to drivers — not filtered through PR, media, or marketing language.
Driver feedback while decisions are still being shaped, not after rollout.

core_themes.theme: Outriders Is a Seat, Not a Perk
core_themes.description: The Outriders Drivers Club as active participation in a new infrastructure model — not a loyalty program or promotional club.

outriders_tie_in.show_connection: Trucker Tuesdays earns trust. Outriders captures the member. Every episode should include one soft CTA toward Outriders — not a pitch, a door. Jeff mentions it once per episode, naturally. Anj includes it once per caption.
outriders_tie_in.approved_cta_line: Want more than just the show? Join the Outriders Drivers Club — free membership, real access.
outriders_tie_in.lead_capture_link: TBD — free membership URL not yet confirmed. FLAG: must be live before any content posts.

visual_identity.logo_status: LOCKED — approved in production. Do not modify.
visual_identity.primary_palette: Chrome, carbon fiber, fuel orange (bright orange with burnt undertones, warm highlights, subtle shadows).
visual_identity.jeff_image_rule: USE JEFF'S ACTUAL PHOTO ONLY. Do not AI-redraw, animate, alter facial features, or modify his expression. Crop chest-up or waist-up depending on layout. He should appear natural, approachable, and confident. If a new photo is needed, take a new photo — do not generate one.

Pull-quote and authority graphics. LHS national freight network map at very low opacity. Reinforces the infrastructure narrative visually.
Alternate/formal version. No scene, no map. Chrome and carbon fiber only. Use when episode tone is more serious or premium.

plan.strategic_context: Trucker Tuesdays is the most direct and human touchpoint in the entire LineHaul Station marketing ecosystem. Every other campaign builds the brand at scale — OneHome, Modern Day Pony Express, Memphis, Boots Walker. Trucker Tuesdays is Jeff speaking directly, weekly, live. The strategic job is twofold: (1) build a recurring driver audience who trusts Jeff's perspective on the industry, and (2) convert that audience into Outriders Drivers Club members with an ongoing relationship to LineHaul Station. The show must never drift into trucker content creator territory. Jeff is a founder, not a host. The content is insider access, not entertainment. The pull-quote graphic is the authority signal that sets this tone before anyone ever presses play. The visual identity is locked and premium — chrome, carbon fiber, fuel orange. The weekly promo graphic is a template, not a new design each episode. Consistency builds the brand. Jeff's real value is in the comments and conversations — that is where credibility is earned and where skeptical drivers become members.

organic_plan_90_day.weekly_focus_notes: Weeks 1–4: Establish the weekly rhythm. Promo post Monday or Tuesday morning. Live Tuesday at 6PM. Clip posted Wednesday. Weeks 5–8: Rotate pull-quote graphic into the mix. Test which creative (promo vs pull-quote) drives more tune-ins. Weeks 9–12: Tie individual episodes to broader campaign narratives — Memphis launch, OneHome, Pony Express. Begin light paid boost on highest-performing episode clips.

initiatives.title: Weekly Promo Post Cadence
next_actions: Post promo graphic Monday morning or Tuesday morning before the 6PM live
Update date and time in the bottom bar field each week — no other changes to the graphic
Use CAP-001 or CAP-002 as caption depending on whether episode has a specific topic
Include Outriders CTA as the final line of every caption — one line only
Post episode clip or recap by Wednesday each week
initiatives.notes: The weekly promo is a template. Consistent look builds brand recognition over time. The caption is where the weekly variation lives, not the graphic.

initiatives.title: Pull Quote Graphic — Final Production
next_actions: Produce square pull-quote graphic per visual_identity.layout_variants.pull_quote spec
Use approved pull-quote copy and attribution from copy_vault
Ghost LHS network map at very low opacity as background
No date/time field — this is evergreen
Once final: post as CNT-002, pin to top of Facebook page, repurpose for LinkedIn
initiatives.notes: This is the single most important static asset in this campaign. It signals authority before any driver watches a single episode. Worth the time to get right.

initiatives.title: Post-Show Clip Workflow
next_actions: Establish clip selection workflow: who pulls it, what turnaround (target: same day or by Wednesday)
Clip the strongest 60–90 second moment from each live
Post as Facebook reel or video post
Use CAP-003 as caption template
Clips extend episode life and drive algorithm reach beyond live viewers — this is not optional
initiatives.notes: Facebook Live streams have limited organic reach after the live ends. The clip is what builds the archive and extends weekly reach. Over time, the clip library becomes the best recruitment tool for new viewers.

initiatives.title: Outriders Drivers Club Integration
next_actions: Confirm Outriders free membership URL — BLOCKER: no CTA can go live without this
Add Outriders CTA as final line of every episode caption (once)
Jeff mentions Outriders once per episode naturally — not as a pitch
Track weekly Outriders sign-ups attributable to the show
initiatives.notes: One mention per post, one mention per episode. The show earns the trust. The club captures the member. More than one mention per post feels like a sales pitch.

initiatives.title: Paid Facebook Boost — Episode Clips
next_actions: Wait for organic baseline — do not boost before Week 6
Identify first clip with strong organic engagement
Boost to: professional drivers, trucking industry, US national
Test $50–100 per boost before committing to recurring spend
Boost clips only — not promo graphics
initiatives.notes: Boosting a clip with existing organic engagement is significantly more efficient than boosting a cold promo post. Paid activates after proof of concept is established organically.

agenda: Confirm date/time is updated in promo graphic
Review last week: live peak viewers, replay views, clip reach, Outriders sign-ups
Flag any comments or questions from last week's live that Jeff should address this week

friday_report.notes: Key metrics: live peak viewers, total live viewers, replay views, clip reach, promo post engagement rate, Outriders CTA clicks, new page follows.

content_queue.caption_or_script: Use CAP-001 from copy_vault. Attach main promo graphic with date/time updated for first episode. This is the launch announcement post.
asset_links: Trucker Tuesdays Main Promo Graphic — date/time field populated for first episode
content_queue.cta: Tune in live — Facebook Live, Tuesday at 6PM. Outriders Drivers Club link.

content_queue.caption_or_script: Use CAP-004 from copy_vault (pull-quote authority post). Attach pull-quote graphic when finalized. PIN THIS POST to the top of the LineHaul Station Facebook page — it is the evergreen show introduction.
asset_links: Trucker Tuesdays Pull Quote Graphic (in production)

content_queue.caption_or_script: Weekly promo template. Use CAP-001 or CAP-002. Update date/time in graphic only. Reuse same graphic template every week.
content_queue.cta: Tune in live — Tuesday at 6PM. Outriders link.

content_queue.publish_date: RECURRING — every Wednesday after the live
content_queue.caption_or_script: Post-show clip. Use CAP-003. 60–90 seconds. Strongest moment from the live. Insert episode takeaway in brackets.
asset_links: Episode clip pulled from Facebook Live replay
content_queue.cta: New episodes every Tuesday at 6PM. Join Outriders. [LINK]

engagement_plan.weekly_targets: During the live: Jeff responds to comments in real time — this is the whole point. After the live: Anj or Jeff responds to top 10 comments within 24 hours. Prioritize questions and skeptical comments over reactions — those are the conversion opportunities. Tag drivers who leave substantive comments for Outriders follow-up.
engagement_plan.notes: The comment section during and after the live is as important as the show itself. A founder who engages honestly with hard questions is what makes this different from every other trucking show. Do not let comments go cold. Critical comments are the most valuable — a confident, thoughtful response to a skeptic in public is worth more than a dozen promo posts.

cross_campaign_connections.CMP-0001_BootsWalker: Boots Walker and Trucker Tuesdays both serve the driver community but in very different registers — Boots is humor and culture, Trucker Tuesdays is authority and insider access. Do not merge the two in the same post. Boots can reference the show in character, but keep the brand voices cleanly separated.
cross_campaign_connections.CMP-0003_OneHome: Trucker Tuesdays is an ideal organic channel for OneHome education. Jeff can explain the OneHome concept live, walk through the financial math, and take driver questions in real time. OneHome-specific episodes should be promoted through CMP-0003 as major content moments.
cross_campaign_connections.CMP-0005_WestMemphis: Trucker Tuesdays episodes focused on the Memphis launch or the national network are crossover events with CMP-0005. These are not regular Tuesday posts — they are live briefings about the location launch and should be promoted accordingly across both campaigns.

First episode date is not yet confirmed — confirm before scheduling any content
Jeff's photo must NOT be AI-redrawn, animated, or altered — use the actual uploaded photo as-is
Post-show clip workflow is not yet established — assign ownership and turnaround time before launch
Weekly promo graphic template is approved — do not redesign week to week, update date/time field only
next_actions: Confirm first Trucker Tuesdays episode date and topic
Confirm Outriders Drivers Club free membership URL — hard blocker
Finalize pull-quote graphic (news/editorial layout per spec)
Update promo graphic with correct date/time for first episode
Schedule CNT-001 (launch promo post) for Monday or Tuesday morning before first episode
Schedule CNT-002 (pull-quote authority post) same week as launch — pin to top of Facebook page
Assign post-show clip workflow: who pulls, who posts, turnaround target
Jeff records one 30-second show open that matches the approved positioning language
notes: Jeff is a founder, not a host. Every design, copy, and content decision should reinforce that framing. The moment this looks like a trucker content show, the positioning is broken.
The pull-quote graphic is the most important single asset in this campaign — it sets the authority tone before anyone watches a single episode. Worth the time to get right.
The weekly promo graphic is a template, not a new design each episode. Consistency is the brand-building mechanism. Only the date/time field changes.
The approved hook — 'You don't know what the truck's going on until you've heard the bold new plan' — is locked. Do not rewrite it week to week. Let it become a recognizable signature line.
The Outriders CTA appears exactly once in every post and once per live episode. More than once is a pitch. Once is an invitation.

Skeptical and critical comments in the live are the highest-value engagement opportunities in this campaign. Jeff engaging honestly with hard questions in public is more persuasive than any promo graphic.
For LinkedIn use or formal placements where the pun may not land, use the approved variant: 'You don't understand where trucking is headed until you've heard what's being built.'
